answer:
# Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

## What It Is

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances Large Language Models (LLMs) by retrieving relevant document chunks from external knowledge bases through semantic similarity calculation [Source: scripts\input\retrieval-augmented-generation-llm-2024-tongji-fundan-universities.pdf, Chunk 71].

## Why It Is Useful

RAG addresses several key limitations of LLMs:

1. **Reduces hallucinations**: By referencing external knowledge, RAG effectively reduces the problem of generating factually incorrect content [Source: scripts\input\retrieval-augmented-generation-llm-2024-tongji-fundan-universities.pdf, Chunk 71].

2. **Handles knowledge-intensive tasks**: LLMs face significant limitations in domain-specific or knowledge-intensive tasks, and RAG helps overcome these challenges [Source: scripts\input\retrieval-augmented-generation-llm-2024-tongji-fundan-universities.pdf, Chunk 71].

3. **Improves real-world applications**: RAG's integration into LLMs has resulted in widespread adoption, establishing it as a key technology in advancing chatbots and enhancing the suitability of LLMs for real-world applications [Source: scripts\input\retrieval-augmented-generation-llm-2024-tongji-fundan-universities.pdf, Chunk 71].
